messages.author: TeamMo8 (Jason)
messages.text: Major US Regions & Cities Pacific Northwest: This area has seen "significant growth" and represents a major hub. Seattle Area, WA: Described as "super amazing" and highly active. Boise, ID: Growing community. Portland, OR: Active, with efforts to connect Portland to Seattle and Vancouver. New England: Boston Metro, MA: Dense network with over 70 repeaters in a 60km area (north-south/east-west). Woburn, MA & Southern NH: High activity near the NH/MA border. Other US Locations: Austin, TX: Known for having a "massive" network, according to user forum reports. Denver, CO: Identified as a node location. Southern California: Active network, though smaller compared to some other regions. Cincinnati, OH.
messages.content_html: Major US Regions & Cities Pacific Northwest: This area has seen "significant growth" and represents a major hub. Seattle Area, WA: Described as "super amazing" and highly active. Boise, ID: Growing community. Portland, OR: Active, with efforts to connect Portland to Seattle and Vancouver. New England: Boston Metro, MA: Dense network with over 70 repeaters in a 60km area (north-south/east-west). Woburn, MA & Southern NH: High activity near the NH/MA border. Other US Locations: Austin, TX: Known for having a "massive" network, according to user forum reports. Denver, CO: Identified as a node location. Southern California: Active network, though smaller compared to some other regions. Cincinnati, OH.

messages.author: TeamMo8 (Jason)
messages.text: Central PA was mostly MT nodes and still is. I am playing with MC and i see some benefits. I think MT has a place.
messages.content_html: Central PA was mostly MT nodes and still is. I am playing with MC and i see some benefits. I think MT has a place.

messages.author: Jonathan
messages.text: me too - I think MT is still very valuable.
messages.content_html: me too - I think MT is still very valuable.

messages.author: bbrodka
messages.text: For large regional networks, MC is the way to go, every MT region that ever grew to any size begins to fail with too much unnecessary traffic, I have never seen a MC convert every switch back to MT IF they join a an established MC network, but you guys have to build some key infrastructure and work together, if you don't keep MC repeaters up because you think no one is around, you'll never establish a decent network. Get repeaters in key locations and work together to expand. You guys aren't that far from us, we will eventually connect And yes. Reliable communication from state college to almost Washington Dc https://mdt.meshmapper.net/
messages.content_html: For large regional networks, MC is the way to go, every MT region that ever grew to any size begins to fail with too much unnecessary traffic, I have never seen a MC convert every switch back to MT IF they join a an established MC network, but you guys have to build some key infrastructure and work together, if you don't keep MC repeaters up because you think no one is around, you'll never establish a decent network. Get repeaters in key locations and work together to expand. You guys aren't that far from us, we will eventually connect And yes. Reliable communication from state college to almost Washington Dc https://mdt.meshmapper.net/
messages.reactions_html: 2

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: the meshcore dev team have moved to a new discord server. details and reasons are here: https://blog.meshcore.io/2026/04/23/the-split Please create a new thread for your region in the https://discord.com/channels/1495203904898728149/1495412453150818585 for your region on the new meshcore.io discord server. https://meshcore.gg
